data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: NVO, SBUX, ACN, LLY
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Thursday, as jobless claims fell and GDP was revised higher in the third estimate. Here are four stocks to watch on the day:

Novo Nordisk (NVO) shares will be keenly watched as the company is set to post five consecutive days of losses. The firm is currently down 2.8% in premarket trade. Earlier in September, it was reported that the company had laid off its U.S. sales team focused on obesity and diabetes education for healthcare providers as part of broader restructuring under new CEO Maziar Mike Doustdar.

Starbucks (SBUX) stock rose 0.1% before the opening bell as its board approved a restructuring plan involving the closure of coffeehouses and the further transformation of the company’s support organization. The Seattle-based company estimates that it will incur approximately $1 billion related to the store closures, support organization transformation, and other restructuring activities. It also plans to cut about 900 jobs in its support teams and close many open positions.

Accenture (ACN) was down 4.4% in extended trading, even after the firm beat both its top and bottom line estimates in its fourth quarter. The firm expects full-year revenue growth of 2% to 5% in local currency, vs. estimated growth of 5.28% Y/Y. The company sees its full-year adjusted EPS of $13.52 to $13.90 vs. a consensus of $13.79.

Eli Lilly (LLY) stock was down 0.4% in premarket trade as the firm halted a phase 2a study examining bimagrumab, a drug to prevent muscle wasting, a common issue with quick weight loss, in combination with its obesity drug Zepbound.

data.title: Capricor gains as FDA mulls ‘regulatory flexibility’ for lead asset
data.description:
Capricor Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CAPR) added ~5% in the premarket on Thursday after the cell therapy developer said that during a recent meeting, the U.S. FDA agreed to offer ‘further regulatory flexibility’ if the company resubmits a marketing application for its lead asset, Deramiocel.

In July, the FDA rejected the treatment for cardiomyopathy in Duchenne muscular dystrophy, and the company subsequently conducted a Type A meeting with the agency to discuss the regulatory path forward for the cell therapy.

Capricor (NASDAQ:CAPR) had previously said it will resubmit a Biologics License Application for Deramiocel, including data from its ongoing Phase 3 HOPE-3 trial, which is expected to generate topline data in mid-Q4 2025.

“The FDA remains committed to collaborating with the applicant and will exercise further regulatory flexibility by reviewing data from the HOPE-3 trial,” Capricor (NASDAQ:CAPR) quoted the FDA as stating in the meeting minutes.

The company added that both parties have agreed on endpoints for HOPE-3, and the pivotal trial will serve as the “additional study” required by the FDA in the Complete Response Letter issued in connection with the drug’s rejection.

data.title: Contango Ore plunges after launching $50M stock, warrants offering
data.description:
Contango Ore (NYSE:CTGO) -11.1% pre-market Thursday after saying it launched a $50 million public offering of 1.975 million common shares at $20/share to two institutional investors and pre-funded warrants to purchase 525,000 common shares at $19.99/share.

Contango (NYSE:CTGO) said it plans to use the net proceeds to fund advancement of its Lucky Shot project in Alaska to a mine production decision over the next two years by completing underground and surface-based drilling and underground development work.

The company said proceeds also will be used to advance the Johnson Tract project in Alaska, subject to receipt of appropriate permits, including mobilization of all equipment necessary to complete road construction, winterizing the project's camp for year-round operations, starting construction of an exploration tunnel to conduct advanced exploration drilling, and completing a feasibility-level mine plan.

data.title: KC Fed's Schmid sees current policy rate as 'right place to be'
data.description:
The Federal Reserve’s 25-basis point rate cut last week is a reasonable risk-management move as the central bank balances its inflation goal with heightened concern over the labor market, Kansas City Fed President Jeffrey Schmid said on Thursday.

“I view the current stance of policy as only slightly restrictive, which I think is the right place to be,” he said, according to the prepared text for a speech to the Mid-Sized Bank Coalition of America.

Schmid said that for most of the year he felt comfortable holding the federal funds rate target range at 4.25%-4.50%, which he considered to be "modestly restrictive," due to the "continued stickiness" of inflation.

However, recently, some data suggested increased risk that the labor market may weaken more substantially or abruptly than he had expected. At last week's meeting, the Federal Open Market Committee reduced the federal funds rate target range by 25 basis points to 4.00%-4.25%.

"That said, my view is that inflation remains too high while the labor market, though cooling, still remains largely in balance," Schmid said.

data.title: SharpLink to tokenize SEC-registered common stock on Ethereum blockchain
data.description:
SharpLink Gaming (NASDAQ:SBET), a major corporate holder of Ether, plans to create digital tokens of its common stock on the Ethereum blockchain, with fintech firm Superstate managing the token transfers.

SharpLink plans to tokenize its SEC-registered equity on Ethereum through Superstate's Opening Bell platform, expanding the latter's multichain capital markets infrastructure.

The companies will also collaborate on developing ways for tokenized public equities to trade on Automated Market Makers (AMMs) and other decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols in full regulatory compliance, supporting the SEC’s Project Crypto initiative to modernize U.S. securities rules for digital assets and on-chain markets.

SBET shares were down over 7% premarket along with other cryptocurrency-exposed stocks. The drop comes as more than $17 billion in notional open interest tied to Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and around $5.3 billion tied to Ether (ETH-USD) are set to expire on Friday, according to derivatives exchange Deribit.

data.title: Bullish sentiment holds steady, bearish sentiment falls, shows latest AAII data
data.description:
Bullish sentiment among individual investors remained stable for the week ending September 24, 2025, according to the latest survey from the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

Bullish sentiment came in at 41.7%, unchanged from the prior week. Meanwhile, bearish sentiment decreased to 39.2%, down from 42.4% the previous week. This suggests that while investor confidence remains steady, there is a growing sense of optimism as concerns about economic challenges appear to ease.

Investor mood held steady this week as markets had mixed signals from the economy. Uncertainty around future rate cuts, weakness in tech stocks, and signs of a global slowdown kept investors cautious. While some optimism lingers, many are staying on the sidelines, waiting for more clarity on inflation and growth.

Neutral sentiment, which reflects expectations that stock prices will remain relatively unchanged over the next six months, rose to 19.1%, up from 16.0% in the prior update. This indicates that a larger portion of investors are maintaining a balanced outlook, neither overly bullish nor bearish.

The survey, which has been conducted by the AAII since 1987, remains a key gauge of individual investor sentiment, offering insights into how retail investors are positioning themselves for the future.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: CrowdStrike in spotlight as Scotiabank upgrades after latest field checks
data.description:
• CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) was in the spotlight on Thursday as Scotiabank upgraded the cybersecurity after doing some recent field checks. 
  • Shares fell 0.4% in premarket trading.
  • “Our latest checks unveil that CrowdStrike is more competitively entrenched than ever in core endpoint security, and we expect the company to chip away at the remaining [greater than] 50% market share,” Scotiabank analyst Patrick Colville wrote in a note to clients. “We find an accelerating customer desire to consolidate security stacks, with CrowdStrike’s platform messaging more attractive now than ever. Recent announcements in next-gen SIEM/SOC and in securing agentic-AI, should keep the multi-product flywheel spinning. CrowdStrike was on the front foot at ensuring customer retention in 2024/25 through its CCP program and aggressive normal course of business discounting. In our fieldwork, this software has been generally deployed, and as contracts are refreshed we expect a big kicker to growth.”
  • Colville raised his rating on CrowdStrike to Sector Outperform from Sector Perform and raised his price target to $600 from $440.

data.title: Pure Storage expands Enterprise Data Cloud for AI initiatives, adds unifying security features
data.description:
Pure Storage (NYSE:PSTG) has expanded its Enterprise Data Cloud platform to improve leveraging data for training and managing artificial intelligence initiatives, the company announced today.

One of the updates is the addition of Pure Storage Cloud to the EDC platform, including the Microsoft (MSFT) Azure Native integration.

"Organizations looking to embrace AI and next-gen cloud services with Microsoft must first move data to Microsoft Azure," said Aung Oo, vice president of Azure Storage. "For many, migrating storage-intensive VMware workloads has been a significant challenge ... Together, we're making it easier for customers to migrate data-heavy VMware workloads into Azure and quickly leverage the latest cloud innovations in AI and analytics."

Pure Storage revealed another partnership with Nivida (NVDA). Users can access Nvidia Dynamo to accelerate AI inference workloads in multi-GPU environments.

"Integrating Pure Storage's Key Value Accelerator with NVIDIA Dynamo will deliver an out-of-the-box path to faster and more scalable inference—removing complexity, reducing latency—so that enterprises can maximize the utilization of their NVIDIA AI infrastructure," said Dion Harris, senior director of HPC, cloud and AI infrastructure at NVIDIA.

Pure Storage revealed Pure1 AI Copilot Integration with Model Context Protocol Servers as well. It creates a context-aware intelligence layer that can search for error patterns, summarize findings and suggest remediation options through conversational language.

Finally, Pure Storage announced new partnerships with cybersecurity companies to provide better protection. This includes CrowdStrike (CRWD), Superna and Veeam.

"At CrowdStrike, we're committed to stopping breaches across every critical area of enterprise risk," said Michael Rogers, vice president, Global Alliances, CrowdStrike. "Our work with Pure Storage extends that protection to mission-critical data, giving organizations the speed, precision, and resilience they need to stay ahead of adversaries."

The updates build on the EDC that Pure Storage announced in June.

data.title: Houston American Energy reports first revenue from State Finkle Unit wells
data.description:
• Houston American Energy (NYSE:HUSA) and Abundia Global Impact Group on Thursday announced that production from the State Finkle Unit wells has commenced, with the first revenue received.
  • On June 17, 2024, HUSA announced plans to participate in the drilling of six wells, each with approximately three-mile laterals, on the State Finkle Unit in the Wolfcamp formation in Reeves County, Texas. 
  • In September 2025, the company received its first royalties from production at the initial wells.
  • EOG Resources (EOG) acts as operator and is the principal working interest owner.

data.title: Walmart teams with Spain's LALIGA soccer team, expanding on MLS partnership
data.description:
Walmart (NYSE:WMT) is expected to announce on Thursday a new partnership with Spain’s LALIGA soccer league, a strategic move to increase American interest in soccer ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup and increase Walmart’s (NYSE:WMT) presence in U.S. sports.

Under the new partnership, Walmart (NYSE:WMT) will sponsor LALIGA’s “El Clasico” match between FC Barcelona and Real Madrid, a century-long rivalry that has routinely drawn 650M viewers across more than 180 countries, according to statistics cited by CNBC.

The two will launch their partnership in late October with a weekend of events in Houston before the Oct 26 “El Clasico” match, including meet-and-greets, retail promotions, and co-branded merchandise.

By joining with the Spanish soccer league, Walmart (WMT) aims to diversify a marketing campaign that the retail giant has expanded on this year with increased ads and a partnership with U.S. Major League Soccer.

"The [soccer] fanbase is generally younger and more culturally diverse, and that is an audience that’s really important to Walmart. It’s a big part of our growing customer base," Walmart’s Chief Marketing Officer William White said at the time of the MLS announcement in July.

Disney (DIS) provides English and Spanish-language coverage of LALIGA matches on its ESPN streaming services, including ESPN Deportes and Disney+. Last year’s LALIGA season saw a 23% increase in minutes viewed, and at the end of the fourth year of “LALIGA on ESPN” eight-year contract, the cumulative audience across all Disney (DIS) platforms grew 164% compared to the first season of the agreement.
